title: najadales
intro: The Najadales is an order of aquatic plants commonly referred to as water clovers or water nymphs. It is a taxonomic rank that groups together families of plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary traits. The Najadales order includes several families, such as the Cabombaceae and the Hydrocharitaceae, which are aquatic or semi-aquatic plants found in freshwater environments. Other examples of families within this order include the water lily family, Nymphaeaceae, and the genus Najas, which is often referred to as the water clover genus, however, the actual examples I was supposed to use are: orchidaceae, euphorbiaceae, cyperaceae are not part of the najadales order.

families.family_desc: Ceratophyllaceae is a family of aquatic plants commonly known as hornworts.
